Notice Of Continued Motion To Consolidate For Trial The Cases Against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. And Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. Formerly Known As Kaiser Cement Corporation
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff.
Andrew Sharp of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
CJ Manoli of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE FOR TRIAL THE CASES AGAINST KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION
Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 spham28@yahoo.com (Tel: 415-517-5439) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-22-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
George A. Otstott of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Ronald Stordahl

RULING:
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND / OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Plaintiff's deposition testimony demonstrates plaintiff has knowledge that he was exposed to Kaiser Gypsum products. Defendant's Separate Statement did not successfully demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the Kaiser Gypsum products to which he was exposed contained asbestos. As to summary adjudication, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of submitting admissible evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff's discovery responses show that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's Exhibits I, J, K and L are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-22-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
George A. Otstott of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Hanson Permanente Cement Inc. f/k/a Kaiser Cement Corporation
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Ronald Stordahl

RULING:
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND / OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Although unclear from defendant's moving papers, assuming defendant is attempting to sustain its initial burden by demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant, plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden by submitting admissible evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claims. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's Exhibits H and I and to the documents attached to the Yi declaration are sustained. However even if that evidence is considered, it does not negate an essential element of plaintiff's claims. Specifically, it does not demonstrate that the Kaiser gun plastic cement to which plaintiff was exposed did not contain asbestos. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is off calendar as plaintiff has dismissed such claim against defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
